pre_game.message: Hey, I'm G — I love games and trying my luck; nice to meet you. Are you here to win or just for the experience?
Mostly to win — I want to bring home money for myself and the people I care about, but I’m here to enjoy the game too.
Nice — same here. I’m determined to win for my people too. Do you have a strategy or are you planning to play it by ear?
I’ve got a loose plan — watch how people act early, team up with a couple of trustworthy players, then press when the odds favor me; but I’ll stay flexible and read the room.
Smart plan — I like the flexible approach. I’m up for watching the room and teaming up when the time’s right; want to keep an eye on a couple of players together?
Sounds good — let’s watch a couple together; want to focus on the loud/confident types and the quiet ones, then decide who we can trust?
Sounds good — let’s watch the loud/confident types and the quiet ones, note who pushes and who avoids risks, and run a small trust test later to see who’s reliable.
Perfect — let’s pick two loud and two quiet to watch, note who pushes or folds, then run a small trust test (like a tiny split or info‑share) later to see who actually follows through.
Sounds great — I’m in. Let’s pick two loud and two quiet as they emerge, watch a few rounds, then offer a small split or share a bit of info to test who really follows through.
Perfect — let’s watch a few rounds, note the two loudest and two quietest, then offer a tiny split to test trust; I’ll watch body language and betting patterns — you ready?
Ready — let’s watch three rounds; I’ll track the loud ones, you watch the quiet, then we’ll meet up to test with a tiny split.
Sounds good — I’ll watch the quiet ones for three rounds, note how they bet and react, then meet you by the corner table to run that tiny split test.
Sounds good — I’ll watch the loud ones for three rounds, meet you at the corner table after, and we’ll run that tiny split; see you there.
Sounds good — see you at the corner table after three rounds; I’ll have the tiny split ready to test trust.
Perfect — see you at the corner table after three rounds; I’ll bring a tiny split and keep an eye out for tells.
Great — see you at the corner table after three rounds; I’ll have the tiny split ready, watch for tells and we’ll compare notes.
Sounds good — see you there; I’ll bring the tiny split and jot down tells so we can compare notes.
Great — sounds like a plan; see you at the corner table after three rounds, bring the split and your notes.
Got it — see you at the corner table after three rounds; I’ll bring the split and my notes so we can compare tells.
Sounds good — see you at the corner table after three rounds; bring the split and notes, we’ll be discreet.
See you there — I’ll be discreet; bring your notes and I’ll have the tiny split ready.
